what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG play icon as a right-pointing filled triangle using currentColor.
observations: Root svg uses viewBox 0 0 24 24 with explicit width and height of 24.
Root styling sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor with 1.5 stroke width, round caps, and round joins.
The visible shape is a polygon with points 8,5 19,12 8,19.
The polygon fills with currentColor and also inherits stroke styling.
standouts: Color is inherited from surrounding text or CSS through currentColor.
The play mark is filled rather than outline-only.
conventions: Uses inline SVG presentation attributes instead of CSS classes.
Keeps the file compact with no metadata, title, or comments.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present for intrinsic accessibility labeling.
Explicit width and height may require overrides when used at other sizes.
notes: A compact currentColor SVG asset for a filled play control icon.
questions: Should consuming markup treat this icon as decorative or provide an accessible label?
Is the filled triangle intentional within the surrounding icon style?
Should the fixed 24px dimensions be preserved or overridden by consumers?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename indicates a play icon, and the SVG content matches that by drawing a right-pointing play triangle.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable play icon that adopts the caller's color and fits a standard 24 by 24 icon grid.
contribution: Contributes a compact, theme-aware play glyph that a fused system would keep as a reusable media-control icon asset.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24 by 24 currentColor SVG play icon made from one filled right-pointing polygon.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a play action in an interface while inheriting color from its usage context.
